posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Belal Muhammad vs Gabriel Bonfim
Pick: Bonfim via decision
26% of Tapology users (78 users) are picking Bonfim (58% decision)
Bonfim is 9.1 years younger (28.8 vs 37.9)
Both fighters have 72” reach
Bonfim has +0.93 strike differential/min vs +0.61
Bonfim has +1.78 strike differential/min @distance vs +0.56
Muhammad proj. +21.7 significant strikes attempted
Bonfim proj. +4.0 significant strikes landed
Muhammad proj. +9.8 head strikes landed
Bonfim proj. 0.42 knockdowns vs 0.20
Bonfim proj. 3.5 takedowns landed (10.9 attempts) vs 3.0 (9.9 attempts)
Bonfim has 14:4 takedown ratio, he spent 24% of fight time in control, 10% being controlled
Muhammad has 46:5 takedown ratio, he spent 24% of fight time in control, 6% being controlled
Bonfim proj. 2.33 submission attempts vs 0.17
Bonfim has 5:0 submission attempt ratio (60% secured, 0% conceded)
Muhammad has 3:1 submission attempt ratio (33% secured, 0% conceded)
Bonfim proj. 44.2% finish chance vs 12.5% and 43.3% decision
Good upside on Bonfim via submission (via decision is safer play)
Bonfim is much younger, will likely win striking and grappling, and is 3.5x more likely to get finish

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Mitchell vs. Henry
Bryce Mitchell, SUB, R2
Bryce Mitchell brother. Arkansas. Jesus. Guns. Mitchell gets rocked round one, grabs Henry takes him down, gets on his back or on top, tires him out, subs him in the second. Bryce hasn't gotten a sub in a minute, but he has been fighting some damn good fighters.

Chaves vs. Duben
Yuneisy Duben, DEC
I don't care.

Leavitt vs. Brito
Jordan Leavitt, DEC
Brito is a fraud. I was on the train, I was so certain the dude was good. He beat Diego Lopes on contender series, he was big and physical, he put BTA on Shore who i rate decently, and then he got exposed. He beat Gomis that **** was a robbery, but good fighters shouldn't even be having close fights with Gomis, Gomis sucks. Getting thouroughly dominated by Pat Sabatini???? Are we for real? In his last fight against a noobdy on short notice from the regional scene he looks like ****? Brito is not that guy. Leavitt has experience, he is going to outgrapple Brito, he is not going to just get outmuscled and finished.

Souza vs. Carnelossi
Ariane Carnelossi, DEC
nobody cares.

Schnell vs. Rodriguez
Imanol Rodriguez, TKO, R1
This is a setup lol. Schnell who between having a glass chin, and being weak on the ground is the probably the most finishable flyweight around, vs a 7-0 prospect that has knocked out his last 4 opponents. UFC wants Mexican prospects badly.

Chandler vs. Cachoeira
Priscila Cachoeira, DEC
We dont care.

McGhee vs. Wikłacz
Marcus McGhee, TKO, R2
Wiklacz's win over Mix was nice. IDK what happened to Mix but credit where it's due. He was getting his **** rocked by Gafurov. Getting absolutely lit up on the feet. Every 25 seconds Gafurov landed a hook or an overhand that spun Wiklacz's chin 90 degrees in either direction. Comparing Gafurov and McGhee, McGhee is a better striker, he is faster, he is more powerful, he is more intelligent in his shot selection, he throws with more output, and he is very solid at staying on the feet. This is a nightmare matchup for Wiklacz, and I do not see it going well for him.

* Edited at 04.22.2026,  1:52 AM ET *

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Muhammad vs. Bonfim
Belal Muhammad, DEC
Belal via cardio and grappling. If Dalby can make you curl up in a ball and quit in the octagon, and in the year 2025 you have to beat Wonderboy by robbery decision, you are not good. Belal had a solid winstreak, I don't think he is this amazing champion, I think he exploited the fact that Leon is one of the worst champions we have ever seen. But even then, Belal is a dog, look at the JDM fight.

Silva vs. Cháirez
Edgar Cháirez, TKO, R2
Banger fight. I like Chairez here. He really impressed me against Van, and Taira even though he lost to both, he had winning moments and even won rounds. Then he cashed in the free win by TKO that is CJ Vergara, and looked a bit rough against Bunes. I do think with his length, power, and athleticism he can get a win here. Bruno will cheat, but recently points are actually getting taken its pretty cool. Bruno will land some big shots, but I have seen Bruno get victimized a few too many times.

Allen vs. Shahbazyan
Brendan Allen, DEC
Terrible matchmaking. Genuinely awful matchmaking. Allen must be getting paid basically nothing if he had to accept this, because I assume the UFC couldn't get Allen someone he deserves like Dricus, Caio, etc. And if Allen was making good money there is no reason to risk losing this. It's a simple ass matchup, if this **** goes to round 2 Allen wins. I'm so scared for him though. Edmen hurts everyone he fights. Everyone. He can finish anyone in round 1. This feels so similar to Sean Brady vs Michael Morales, except Shabahzyan is not half the anti grappler Morales is.

Baraniewski vs. Elekana
Iwo Baraniewski, TKO, R1
Elekana is a can. He got hit by Guskov who is a big hitter, and just quit. Covered up and offered his neck. Iwo is a big hitter. He can make Elekana give up the same way. To be clear I don't buy the Iwo hype. Knocking out Austen Lane isn't impressive. Getting rocked 3 times by Ibo Aslan is not a good thing, Ibo is terrible.

Ziam vs. Nolan
Fares Ziam, DEC
I really want Nolan to win because it would be funny. Ziam is just so massive for lightweight. He's one of the biggest weight bullies in the division. I don't really buy the hype, I think Ziam is not going to do well against the ranked lightweights that keep destroying the unranked guys. I do think he beats Nolan though, just pushes him against the cage, gets takedowns when he wants, pitter patter shots at range.

posts.author: battousai
posts.content:
Marcus McGhee went from being ranked #12 to not being ranked at all after losing to the current champ. Next fight is a prelim fight in the Apex against an unranked guy.

Meanwhile Chito Vera hasn't won a fight in 3 years, has gotten 3 ranked opponents since losing to the (at the time) champion in OMalley, and is still (somehow) ranked in the top 10 despite losing every one of those fights.

I got nothing.

posts.author: The Giraffe
posts.content:
Vera should have been 15 before the most recent loss, and gone afterwards.  Marcus McGhee should never have been ranked.  The whole "you beat X you take their spot" way of doing things is hot garbage, and inferior to other methods, like a points-based system.  My $0.02.

/Ted Talk
